Interface ProofScriptCommand

All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractCommand, ActivateCommand, AllCommand, AssertCommand, AssumeCommand, AutoCommand, AxiomCommand, CutCommand, EchoCommand, ExitCommand, FocusCommand, HideCommand, InstantiateCommand, JavascriptCommand, LeaveCommand, LetCommand, MacroCommand, NoArgumentCommand, RewriteCommand, RuleCommand, SaveInstCommand, SaveNewNameCommand, SchemaVarCommand, ScriptCommand, SelectCommand, SetCommand, SetEchoCommand, SetFailOnClosedCommand, SkipCommand, SMTCommand, TryCloseCommand, UnhideCommand
@NullMarked public interface ProofScriptCommand

A ProofScriptCommand is an executable mutation on the given proof. It abstracts complex operations, and made them accessible for an API.

ProofScriptCommand are supported by the java.util.ServiceLoader. You can add new proof script commands by add a new entry to META-INF/service/de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.scripts.ProofScriptCommand.

Version 2 (2017-03-28): change of the interface support for structured arguments.

  • Method Details

    • getArguments

      List<ProofScriptArgument> getArguments()
      Self-documentation of this command by returning a list of descriptions for arguments.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of ProofScriptArgument

    • execute

      void execute(AbstractUserInterfaceControl uiControl, ScriptCommandAst args, EngineState stateMap) throws ScriptException, InterruptedException
      Parameters:
      uiControl - the current ui controller
      args - the script arguments
      stateMap - the current state
      Throws:
      ScriptException - if something bad happens
      InterruptedException - if something bad happens

    • getName

      String getName()
      Returns the name of this proof command. The name should be constant and not be clash with the name of other commands. The name is essential for finding this command within an hashmap.
      Returns:
      a non-null, non-empty string
      See Also:
      • ProofScriptEngine

    • getAliases

      default List<String> getAliases()

      Announce a list of potential aliases of this command.

      The command can react differently for each alias. The call name is given to execute(AbstractUserInterfaceControl,ScriptCommandAst,EngineState) inside ScriptCommandAst. For example LetCommand announce letf as an alias for an overwriting binding, otherwise error are thrown on re-binding.

      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of alias names under which command can be called, including getName()

    • getDocumentation

      String getDocumentation()
      A documentation for the commands.
      Returns:
      a non-null string
